methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
KoBold Metals, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Kurt House and Jeff Satterfield, has raised over $400M and carries a post-money valuation of $2.0B+. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA), including peers such as Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), Amogy ($700M, Series C), and Redwood Materials ($6.0B, Series D).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $2.0B last post-money valuation, applies a typical Series C-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.7x, implying $3.4B. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company climate and energy cohort yields a median valuation of $500M, with a range spanning $120M to $6.0B, reflecting the wide dispersion in stage and maturity across the peer set.

The triangulated analysis produces a valuation range of $425M (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The current $2.0B+ post-money sits near the middle of this range. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of peer comparables given heterogeneous technology risk profiles and development timelines within frontier energy, as well as the applicability of stage-progression multiples in a volatile market for early-stage climate capital.
